channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers in Central & Eastern Europe
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Apply for the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026 and Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town Young leaders across Central and Eastern Europe now have the chance to access one of the most prestigious global leadership opportunities through the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026. This fully funded scholarship will support 13 outstanding young […]

The post Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers in Central & Eastern Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Apply for the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026 and Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town

Young leaders across Central and Eastern Europe now have the chance to access one of the most prestigious global leadership opportunities through the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026. This fully funded scholarship will support 13 outstanding young changemakers to attend the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa, from 3–6 November 2026.

If you are passionate about transforming the future of finance, business, sustainability, technology, or economic development, this opportunity could be the next major step in your leadership journey.

What is the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026?

The Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship was created to empower talented youth in Central and Eastern Europe who are shaping solutions to today’s financial and societal challenges. As economies evolve rapidly, many communities still face barriers such as financial exclusion, limited literacy, and unequal access to opportunities.

This scholarship seeks to equip future leaders with global exposure, mentorship, and international networks so they can return home and create lasting impact.

Through this initiative, selected scholars will join the global One Young World Community, a network of more than 22,000 young leaders from over 190 countries.

Scholarship Benefits

Successful applicants will receive:

  • A fully funded place at the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town
  • Access to a global network of young leaders and professionals
  • Lifetime membership in the One Young World community
  • Leadership development opportunities
  • Structured mentorship programs such as the Action Accelerator
  • International exposure and collaboration opportunities
  • Skills, insights, and connections to drive change in local communities

Who Should Apply?

This scholarship is ideal for ambitious young people working or studying in areas such as:

  • Finance
  • Business Analytics
  • Economics
  • Mathematics
  • Statistics
  • Technology
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Public Policy
  • Research
  • Community Development

Applicants should also have a strong interest in solving challenges related to:

  • Financial inclusion
  • Sustainable finance
  • Economic resilience
  • Societal development
  • Long-term value creation

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be aged 18 to 30 at the time of the Summit
  • Be from or actively contributing in eligible Central European countries
  • Demonstrate leadership potential and community impact
  • Show strong academic achievement or professional excellence
  • Possess analytical skills, innovation, and intellectual curiosity
  • Be committed to creating positive change in society
  • Submit truthful and non-discriminatory applications

Applicants over 30 may still be considered if they demonstrate exceptional impact and leadership.

Eligible Countries

Applications are open to candidates from:

  • Austria
  • Bulgaria
  • Croatia
  • Czech Republic
  • Hungary
  • Poland
  • Romania
  • Slovakia

Why You Should Apply

The Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship is more than financial support. It is a gateway to global leadership, lifelong mentorship, and the opportunity to collaborate with changemakers from around the world.

For young leaders in Central and Eastern Europe, this is a rare chance to gain visibility, strengthen your voice, and help shape the future of finance and society.

Application Deadline

  • Opening Date: 7 April 2026
  • Deadline: 18 May 2026

Early application is strongly recommended.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official One Young World scholarship portal.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here. 

Final Thoughts

If you are building solutions in finance, business, technology, or sustainability and want to expand your impact globally, the Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026 is an opportunity you should not miss.

This could be your chance to represent your country, connect with world leaders, and become part of a movement creating a better future.

The post Allianz CE MoveNow Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers in Central & Eastern Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FCI4Africa Open Call 2026: Get Up to €50,000 Funding for Food System Innovation Projects in Africa
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Innovators, researchers, start-ups, and technology-driven organizations now have a major opportunity to secure funding for transformative food system solutions in Africa. The FCI4Africa Open Call 1 (OC1) has officially launched, offering up to €50,000 per project to selected applicants working on innovative ideas that strengthen fairness, resilience, and sustainability across African food value chains. Backed […]

The post FCI4Africa Open Call 2026: Get Up to €50,000 Funding for Food System Innovation Projects in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Innovators, researchers, start-ups, and technology-driven organizations now have a major opportunity to secure funding for transformative food system solutions in Africa. The FCI4Africa Open Call 1 (OC1) has officially launched, offering up to €50,000 per project to selected applicants working on innovative ideas that strengthen fairness, resilience, and sustainability across African food value chains.

Backed by the Horizon Europe programme, FCI4Africa is focused on building more inclusive food systems by supporting practical innovations, research-driven tools, and scalable technologies that solve key challenges across the continent.

With a total funding pool of €400,000, eight selected sub-projects will receive financial support to test, validate, and scale impactful solutions.

What is FCI4Africa Open Call 1?

FCI4Africa is a four-year international initiative running from 2024 to 2028 with 14 partners across 7 countries. Its mission is to improve food systems in Africa through innovation, fairness, and resilience.

The first Open Call is designed to attract forward-thinking applicants who can help solve food system challenges through technology, research, and new business models.

Applicants are invited to submit projects aligned with one of the following objectives:

Objective 1: Improve Existing FCI4Africa Tools and Concepts

Applicants can test, validate, and enhance existing business concepts, digital tools, and project use cases already developed within FCI4Africa.

Objective 2: Develop New Solutions for Specific Challenges

Applicants may also propose fresh ideas, tools, or technologies that directly address identified food system challenges under the FCI4Africa framework.

All selected projects are expected to generate useful datasets that can further improve project tools, methods, and business models.

Funding Details

  • Maximum funding per project: €50,000
  • Total call budget: €400,000
  • Number of projects to be funded: 8
  • Funding source: Horizon Europe

This funding can help innovators pilot technologies, validate business ideas, gather evidence, and demonstrate measurable impact.

Project Timeline

The selected projects will run from November 2026 to October 2027 through a structured three-phase implementation process:

Phase 1 – Design

November 2026 – January 2027
Duration: 3 Months
Deliverable: Activity Plan

Phase 2 – Development

February 2027 – July 2027
Duration: 6 Months
Deliverable: Results Demonstration

Phase 3 – Validation

August 2027 – October 2027
Duration: 3 Months
Deliverable: Dissemination Report and Lessons Learned

Who Can Apply?

Applications are open to individual organizations or entities only. Consortia are not allowed.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Universities
  • Research institutes
  • Technology providers
  • R&D SMEs
  • Innovation start-ups
  • Industry actors building new technologies
  • Multidisciplinary stakeholders

Why You Should Apply

This opportunity offers more than funding. It provides a pathway to test solutions in real-world markets, contribute to Africa’s development, and collaborate with a leading international innovation network.

Key Benefits

  • Access up to €50,000 in project funding
  • Gain recognition through a Horizon Europe-backed programme
  • Validate your innovation in practical environments
  • Contribute to sustainable food systems in Africa
  • Build datasets and evidence for future scaling
  • Connect with global experts and innovation partners

How to Apply

Applicants must submit proposals online through the official Open Call platform.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more grant opportunities, click here. 

Deadline

June 30, 2026 at 17:00 CEST (Brussels Time)

Late applications will not be considered, so early submission is strongly advised.

Final Thoughts

The FCI4Africa Open Call 2026 is a high-value opportunity for innovators committed to solving Africa’s food system challenges. Whether you are a start-up building agri-tech solutions, a university conducting research, or an SME creating sustainable tools, this grant can help bring your ideas to life.

If your project can improve fairness, resilience, and inclusivity in food value chains, now is the time to apply.

The post FCI4Africa Open Call 2026: Get Up to €50,000 Funding for Food System Innovation Projects in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Your Green Action Fellowship 2026: Fully Online Climate Leadership Program for Young Changemakers Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Young people around the world are increasingly concerned about climate change, sustainability, and the future of the planet. However, many struggle to find practical ways to make a real difference in their daily lives. The Your Green Action Fellowship 2026 was created to solve that challenge by helping youth transform climate awareness into meaningful action. […]

The post Your Green Action Fellowship 2026: Fully Online Climate Leadership Program for Young Changemakers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people around the world are increasingly concerned about climate change, sustainability, and the future of the planet. However, many struggle to find practical ways to make a real difference in their daily lives. The Your Green Action Fellowship 2026 was created to solve that challenge by helping youth transform climate awareness into meaningful action.

This fully online environmental fellowship is designed for young people who want to learn, grow, and take practical steps toward sustainability. Through engaging lessons, weekly activities, and a supportive global community, participants will gain the tools to create positive change in their homes, schools, workplaces, and communities.

If you are passionate about the environment and want to move from concern to action, this fellowship is the perfect opportunity.

What is the Your Green Action Fellowship?

The Your Green Action Fellowship is a structured online program focused on practical climate action. Rather than teaching theory alone, the fellowship helps participants understand how their everyday choices affect the environment and how small actions can create lasting impact.

Participants will explore key issues such as food systems, waste, energy use, transportation, climate justice, sustainable consumption, and advocacy.

The program also encourages reflection, habit-building, leadership, and storytelling to help fellows inspire change in others.

Program Benefits

Participants selected for the fellowship will receive:

  • Access to short and practical learning sessions
  • Weekly activities focused on real-life climate action
  • Educational resources and curated materials
  • Community experience with like-minded young people worldwide
  • Leadership and advocacy development opportunities
  • A personal Green Action Plan for long-term impact
  • Certificate of Completion after successfully finishing the program
  • Flexible self-paced learning format

What You Will Learn

During the fellowship, participants will gain knowledge and practical skills in the following areas:

  • Climate change and its connection to everyday habits
  • Environmental impact of food, fashion, waste, and energy
  • Sustainable habits that are realistic and accessible
  • Climate justice and global inequalities
  • Reducing your personal environmental footprint
  • Influencing others through positive leadership
  • Storytelling for environmental advocacy
  • Creating long-term sustainability goals

Fellowship Journey

The program covers a step-by-step learning journey, including:

  • Climate Change & Your Role
  • Water Conservation
  • Sustainable Food Choices
  • Ethical Fashion
  • Energy Awareness
  • Waste Reduction
  • Technology & Digital Sustainability
  • Transportation & Mobility
  • Conscious Consumption
  • Community Action & Leadership
  • Climate Storytelling & Advocacy
  • Personal Green Action Plan & Graduation

Why This Fellowship Stands Out

Many climate programs focus heavily on information but provide little guidance for real-life implementation. The Your Green Action Fellowship stands out because it is action-oriented.

Participants do not just learn about climate problems. They build sustainable habits, join a global network, and gain confidence to lead by example.

This combination of education, action, and community makes the fellowship ideal for youth who want practical environmental impact.

Who Should Apply?

This fellowship is ideal for:

  • Students
  • Young professionals
  • Youth leaders
  • Climate beginners
  • Sustainability advocates
  • Community changemakers

No prior environmental knowledge is required.

Global Perspective

The fellowship recognizes that climate change affects regions differently, especially communities in the Global South that often face the greatest challenges despite contributing the least to emissions.

Participants will gain a broader understanding of global inequalities while connecting environmental issues to their local realities.

About the Organizers

Your Green Action is an initiative of the Wang and Tangang Foundation, an international NGO committed to advancing the Sustainable Development Goals through youth empowerment, environmental responsibility, and global citizenship.

The organization’s vision is reflected in its slogan:

Inspiring Action, Impacting Generations.

Application Deadline

The official application deadline is 26 April 2026. Applicants are encouraged to apply early.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the fellowship application carefully. Selected participants will be contacted via email.

Apply now and begin your journey toward a greener future.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here. 

The post Your Green Action Fellowship 2026: Fully Online Climate Leadership Program for Young Changemakers Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026 (Virtual – USA): Early Career Opportunity for First-Year Students in Financial Services
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Are you a first-year university student looking to explore a future in finance, banking, or global business? The Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026 is a highly valuable early-career opportunity designed to introduce students to the world of financial services through interactive workshops, networking sessions, and mentorship. This virtual program provides a structured platform for first-year […]

The post Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026 (Virtual – USA): Early Career Opportunity for First-Year Students in Financial Services appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a first-year university student looking to explore a future in finance, banking, or global business? The Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026 is a highly valuable early-career opportunity designed to introduce students to the world of financial services through interactive workshops, networking sessions, and mentorship.

This virtual program provides a structured platform for first-year students to gain exposure, build foundational knowledge, and begin shaping their professional journey with one of the world’s leading financial institutions.

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by:

April 27, 2026 (9:00 AM EST)

Late submissions will not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to apply early.

Program Dates and Format

  • Program Dates: May 19 – May 21, 2026
  • Format: Virtual
  • Time: Begins at 10:00 AM EST

The program is fully online, making it accessible to eligible students across the United States.

Overview of the Citi Freshman Discovery Program

The Citi Freshman Discovery Program is a virtual workshop series designed to help first-year college students:

  • Explore career opportunities in financial services
  • Gain technical and professional knowledge
  • Build networks with industry professionals
  • Develop clarity on career pathways early in their academic journey

Participants will engage directly with Citi’s Early Career team, gaining insight into real-world financial careers.

What Participants Will Experience

Selected students will take part in a dynamic and interactive learning experience.

Key Program Components:

  1. Virtual Workshops
    • Learn foundational concepts in financial services
    • Explore various career paths within Citi
  2. Networking Opportunities
    • Connect with professionals across Citi
    • Engage with peers and like-minded students
  3. Leadership Engagement
    • Participate in sessions with senior leaders
    • Gain insights into industry trends and expectations
  4. Career Development Sessions
    • Learn about early career planning
    • Develop professional and personal growth strategies

Program Benefits

The Citi Freshman Discovery Program offers a strong foundation for students at the beginning of their careers.

Key Benefits Include:

  1. Early Career Exposure
    • Understand the financial services industry at an early stage
  2. Professional Networking
    • Build relationships with industry experts and peers
  3. Skill Development
    • Gain technical knowledge and soft skills
  4. Mentorship Opportunities
    • Learn directly from Citi professionals
  5. Career Clarity
    • Discover potential career paths and interests

Eligibility Criteria

Academic Requirements:

Applicants must:

  • Be a first-year student currently enrolled in:
    • A four-year Bachelor’s degree program at an accredited US institution
      OR
    • A community college with plans to transfer to a four-year institution

Graduation Timeline:

  • Expected graduation between Fall 2028 and Spring 2029

Ideal Candidate Profile

Citi is looking for students who demonstrate:

  • Strong interest in financial services
  • Curiosity and willingness to learn
  • Teamwork and collaboration skills
  • Initiative and motivation for personal growth

Importantly, the program is open to students from all academic disciplines, including those with limited or no prior exposure to finance.

Selection Criteria

Applicants will be evaluated based on:

  • Academic performance
  • Leadership potential
  • Extracurricular involvement
  • Demonstrated interest in career development

A strong, well-prepared resume is required as part of the application.

Why You Should Apply

The Citi Freshman Discovery Program is a strategic stepping stone for students aiming to build careers in finance, banking, consulting, or related fields.

What Makes This Opportunity Valuable:

  • Provides early exposure to a competitive industry
  • Offers access to global professionals and mentors
  • Enhances your resume with a reputable brand
  • Helps you make informed career decisions early

How to Apply

To apply:

  1. Prepare an updated resume
  2. Ensure you meet all eligibility requirements
  3. Submit your application before the deadline

Applicants are encouraged to highlight leadership experience, curiosity, and interest in financial services.

APPLY HERE

Click HERE to learn more about the Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026.

For more global Conference opportunities, visit Opportunities for Youth HERE.

The post Citi Freshman Discovery Program 2026 (Virtual – USA): Early Career Opportunity for First-Year Students in Financial Services appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: JPO in Political Affairs at the United Nations (DPPA) 2026: Fully Funded Entry-Level Opportunity in New York for Young Professionals
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Are you an aspiring global professional looking to build a career in international relations, peacebuilding, or diplomacy? The Junior Professional Officer (JPO) in Political Affairs position with the United Nations Department of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs (DPPA) presents a highly competitive and prestigious opportunity for young professionals from eligible countries. Hosted in New York, USA, […]

The post JPO in Political Affairs at the United Nations (DPPA) 2026: Fully Funded Entry-Level Opportunity in New York for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you an aspiring global professional looking to build a career in international relations, peacebuilding, or diplomacy? The Junior Professional Officer (JPO) in Political Affairs position with the United Nations Department of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs (DPPA) presents a highly competitive and prestigious opportunity for young professionals from eligible countries.

Hosted in New York, USA, this program provides hands-on experience in global peace and security efforts, particularly within the Gender, Peace, and Security (WPS) agenda.

Overview of the JPO Programme in Political Affairs

The Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme is designed to provide early-career professionals with practical experience in international organizations, particularly within the United Nations system.

This specific opportunity is hosted under the DPPA’s Policy and Mediation Division, focusing on Gender, Peace, and Security (WPS)—a critical global agenda that promotes gender equality in peace processes and conflict resolution.

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by:

April 26, 2026

Late or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Location and Hosting Organization

  • Location: New York, United States
  • Organization: United Nations – Department of Political and Peacebuilding Affairs (DPPA)
  • Hosting Unit: Gender, Peace and Security Unit

This position places selected candidates at the heart of global diplomacy and policymaking.

Key Responsibilities of the JPO

Selected candidates will contribute to high-level international work in peacebuilding and gender equality.

Core Duties Include:

  1. Conflict Prevention and Peacebuilding Support
    • Assist in country-level initiatives related to peacebuilding and peacemaking
    • Support Women, Peace, and Security (WPS) programs
  2. Research and Policy Analysis
    • Monitor global trends in political affairs and gender issues
    • Conduct research and surveys to support UN operations
  3. Reporting and Documentation
    • Contribute to reports submitted to the UN Security Council
    • Support inputs for the Secretary-General’s reports
    • Assist in reporting frameworks such as SWAP 3.0
  4. Data Collection and Monitoring
    • Support data-driven monitoring of WPS initiatives
    • Assist in evaluating program outcomes
  5. Strategic Communication
    • Draft analytical notes, concept papers, and briefing materials
    • Prepare talking points and proposals
  6. Capacity Building and Training
    • Help organize training sessions on gender and peacebuilding
    • Support knowledge-sharing initiatives
  7. Partnerships and Coordination
    • Engage with internal and external stakeholders
    • Support inter-agency collaboration efforts

Eligibility Criteria

This opportunity is highly selective and open only to candidates from specific countries.

Nationality Requirement:

Applicants must be nationals of one of the following eligible countries:

  • Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Benin, Burkina Faso, Burundi
  • Chad, Colombia, DR Congo, Ethiopia, Ghana
  • India, Indonesia, Iraq, Ivory Coast, Jordan
  • Kenya, Lebanon, Mali, Mozambique, Niger
  • Nigeria, Palestinian Territory, Senegal, Somalia
  • South Sudan, Sudan, Tunisia, Uganda, Ukraine
  • Vietnam, Yemen

Learn more

Age Requirement:

  • Maximum age: 32 years
  • Applicants must be born on or after January 1, 1994

Educational Requirements:

Applicants must meet one of the following:

  1. Advanced Degree Pathway
    • Master’s degree (or equivalent) in:
      • International Relations
      • Law
      • Political Science
      • Public Administration
      • Human Rights
      • Gender Studies or related fields
    • Minimum of 2 years relevant work experience
  2. Alternative Pathway
    • Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent)
    • Minimum of 4 years relevant work experience

Additional Academic Requirement:

  • A significant portion of academic studies must have been completed in an eligible country

Required Skills and Competencies

Successful applicants are expected to demonstrate:

  • Strong analytical and research skills
  • Excellent written and verbal communication
  • Knowledge of international affairs and global governance
  • Ability to work in multicultural environments
  • Passion for gender equality and peacebuilding

Click HERE to read the job description.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The JPO Programme offers a rare opportunity to gain direct exposure to the United Nations system and global policymaking processes.

Key Benefits:

  1. Professional Experience at the UN
    • Work within one of the most influential global institutions
  2. Career Advancement
    • Build a strong foundation for careers in diplomacy, international development, and policy
  3. Global Networking
    • Connect with experts, policymakers, and international stakeholders
  4. Hands-on Learning
    • Engage in real-world projects that impact global peace and security

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Carefully review Eligibility Criteria and requirements
  2. Prepare all necessary documents
  3. Submit application through the Official Platform

Applicants are strongly advised to read the FAQ and selection criteria before applying.

Learn more about the JPO in Political Affairs at the United Nations (DPPA) 2026 HERE.

Visit Opportunities for Youth for more global Job opportunities.

The post JPO in Political Affairs at the United Nations (DPPA) 2026: Fully Funded Entry-Level Opportunity in New York for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Clean Economy Bridge Research Program 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders in Canada, USA & Germany
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The transition to a clean and sustainable global economy requires bold ideas, collaborative research, and forward-thinking leadership. The Clean Economy Bridge Research Program 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for young leaders to actively contribute to shaping climate policy, energy systems, and transatlantic cooperation. Delivered by Student Energy in partnership with global stakeholders, this program equips […]

The post Clean Economy Bridge Research Program 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders in Canada, USA & Germany appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The transition to a clean and sustainable global economy requires bold ideas, collaborative research, and forward-thinking leadership. The Clean Economy Bridge Research Program 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for young leaders to actively contribute to shaping climate policy, energy systems, and transatlantic cooperation.

Delivered by Student Energy in partnership with global stakeholders, this program equips emerging researchers with the tools, mentorship, and collaborative platform needed to drive impactful change across North America and Europe.

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by:

April 26, 2026, at 11:59 PM CEST

Applicants are encouraged to apply early to ensure adequate preparation and submission.

What is the Clean Economy Bridge Research Program?

The Clean Economy Bridge Research Program is a transatlantic initiative designed to strengthen collaboration between Canada, the United States, and Germany on clean energy and climate policy.

This program focuses on empowering young leaders to conduct policy-relevant, collaborative research on key issues shaping the global clean economy.

Program Objectives:

  • Advance transatlantic cooperation on climate and energy
  • Equip youth with research, policy, and leadership skills
  • Generate innovative, evidence-based solutions for the clean economy
  • Foster collaboration between emerging leaders and global experts

What Participants Will Do

Selected participants will be part of a cohort of 12–15 young leaders working on cutting-edge research projects.

Key Activities:

  1. Conduct Collaborative Research
    Participants will work in teams to explore pressing global challenges such as:
    • AI and energy systems
    • Energy and agriculture interactions
    • Climate policy development
    • Renewable energy and grid integration
  2. Engage in Policy-Oriented Work
    • Develop actionable policy recommendations
    • Analyze real-world energy and climate challenges
  3. Participate in Learning Modules
    • Access structured learning sessions
    • Gain insights from industry experts
  4. Receive Mentorship
    • Work closely with experienced professionals
    • Gain career guidance and technical support

Research Themes and Focus Areas

Participants will explore high-impact topics shaping the future of energy and sustainability.

1. Energy & Agriculture

  • Investigating competition over water resources between agriculture and AI infrastructure
  • Exploring solutions for sustainable water use
  • Identifying policy frameworks for collaboration

2. Renewable Energy & Grid Integration

  • Studying the impact of data center growth on energy grids
  • Analyzing solar-plus-storage solutions
  • Evaluating grid reliability and scalability

3. Policy Innovation & Just Transition

  • Examining transition strategies for fossil fuel-dependent economies
  • Assessing reskilling programs and community impacts
  • Bridging European and North American policy frameworks

Program Benefits

Participants gain access to a wide range of professional and academic advantages:

  1. Global Research Experience
    • Work on real-world climate and energy challenges
  2. Mentorship and Expert Guidance
    • Learn from leaders in energy, policy, and sustainability
  3. International Collaboration
    • Engage with peers across multiple countries
  4. Skill Development
    • Build research, analytical, and policy-making skills
  5. Career Advancement
    • Strengthen your profile for global opportunities

Eligibility Criteria

Age Requirement:

  • Applicants must be between 18 and 30 years old

Geographic Requirement:

  • Must demonstrate a strong connection to:
    • Canada
    • United States
    • Germany

This connection may include:

  • Citizenship
  • Residence
  • Academic background
  • Professional experience
  • Demonstrated interest in these regions

Educational Background:

Applicants from diverse disciplines are encouraged, including:

  • Environmental science
  • Energy systems
  • Engineering
  • Economics and business
  • Political science and international relations

Non-traditional educational backgrounds are also welcome, including:

  • Vocational training
  • Trade schools
  • Alternative certifications

Program Commitment

Participants are expected to:

  • Commit approximately 5 hours per week
  • Participate in virtual collaboration sessions
  • Actively contribute to research and discussions

Additional Selection Considerations

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong interest in climate and energy innovation
  • Commitment to youth leadership
  • Ability to collaborate across cultures
  • Analytical and research capabilities

Why You Should Apply

This program is more than a research opportunity—it is a platform to influence the future of global energy systems.

Participants will:

  • Gain international exposure
  • Work on impactful, policy-driven projects
  • Build a strong global network
  • Position themselves as future leaders in climate action

How to Apply

Applicants should:

  1. Review eligibility and program requirements carefully
  2. Prepare all required information
  3. Submit their application before the deadline

Applications are competitive, so a strong demonstration of interest and experience in clean energy and climate policy is essential.

APPLY HERE

Click HERE to learn more about the Clean Economy Bridge Research Program 2026.

Discover more other global Fellowship and Research opportunities on Opportunities for Youth.

The post Clean Economy Bridge Research Program 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders in Canada, USA & Germany appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: LEVANTE 2026 Call for Proposals: Up to $1 Million Funding for Global Research on Children’s Learning and Development
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Jacobs Foundation has officially launched the LEVANTE 2026 Call for Proposals, offering a major funding opportunity for researchers worldwide. With a total funding pool of USD 6 million and up to USD 1 million available per project, this initiative aims to support cutting-edge research that explores how children grow, learn, and develop across diverse […]

The post LEVANTE 2026 Call for Proposals: Up to $1 Million Funding for Global Research on Children’s Learning and Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Jacobs Foundation has officially launched the LEVANTE 2026 Call for Proposals, offering a major funding opportunity for researchers worldwide. With a total funding pool of USD 6 million and up to USD 1 million available per project, this initiative aims to support cutting-edge research that explores how children grow, learn, and develop across diverse contexts globally.

This call is part of the Learning Variability Network Exchange (LEVANTE), a groundbreaking global research initiative designed to deepen understanding of learning variability and its impact on children’s educational outcomes.

What is LEVANTE?

LEVANTE (Learning Variability Network Exchange) is a flagship initiative of the Jacobs Foundation that connects researchers across the globe to collaboratively study how children learn and develop.

The initiative focuses on:

  • Individual variability: Differences in how children learn and develop
  • Group heterogeneity: Variations across different populations and communities
  • Contextual variability: Influence of environment, culture, and systems on learning

LEVANTE has already created one of the first cross-cultural, multidisciplinary open datasets on child development, helping researchers uncover patterns and insights that can transform education systems globally.

Overview of the 2026 Call for Proposals

The 2026 call represents a significant expansion of LEVANTE’s global research network.

Key Highlights:

  • Total funding pool: USD 6 million
  • Maximum funding per project: Up to USD 1 million
  • Application period: April 13 – June 10, 2026

This funding is designed to support innovative, high-impact research projects that contribute to a deeper understanding of how learning unfolds across time and contexts.

Research Focus Areas

LEVANTE is specifically seeking research proposals that investigate children’s learning and development through rigorous, data-driven approaches.

Target Population:

  • Children aged 3 to 12 years

Core Research Themes:

  1. Understanding Learning Variability
    • Investigating differences in how children learn
    • Identifying underlying mechanisms of development
  2. Longitudinal Development Studies
    • Tracking children’s development over time
    • Capturing dynamic learning processes
  3. Environmental and Contextual Influences
    • Effects of socio-economic conditions
    • Impact of pollution and environmental factors
    • Role of institutional and policy reforms

Priority Research Approaches

LEVANTE strongly encourages proposals that adopt advanced and rigorous methodologies.

Preferred Approaches Include:

  • Causal Research Designs
    • Studies that identify cause-and-effect relationships in learning and development
  • Dense Data Collection
    • High-frequency data tracking to understand short-term and long-term outcomes
  • Developmental Trajectory Analysis
    • Research focusing on how children evolve over time
  • Use of Rich Environmental Data
    • Integrating contextual variables into learning models

Special Focus: Underrepresented Regions

The 2026 call places strong emphasis on expanding global representation.

Priority Consideration:

  • Projects from underrepresented regions
  • Research that captures diverse cultural and socio-economic contexts

This ensures that findings reflect global realities and contribute to inclusive educational advancements.

Funding Benefits

Selected projects will receive substantial support to implement their research.

What the Funding Covers:

  1. Financial Support
    • Up to USD 1 million per project
  2. Global Research Collaboration
    • Access to an international network of researchers
  3. Data Integration Opportunities
    • Contribution to a global open dataset
  4. Visibility and Impact
    • Opportunity to influence global education policy and research

Expansion of the LEVANTE Network

The initiative continues to grow rapidly, strengthening its global reach.

New Research Sites (2025 Cohort):

  • Switzerland – Bern University of Teacher Education
  • Peru – GRADE
  • USA – Harvard University
  • Ghana – Kintampo Health Research Centre
  • Australia – Monash University
  • India – Public Health Foundation of India
  • Italy – Sapienza University of Rome
  • Colombia – Universidad de los Andes

This expansion highlights LEVANTE’s commitment to global collaboration and knowledge sharing.

Why You Should Apply

The LEVANTE 2026 Call for Proposals offers a rare opportunity to contribute to global knowledge on child development.

Key Reasons to Apply:

  • Access up to $1 million in research funding
  • Join a global network of leading researchers
  • Contribute to groundbreaking datasets
  • Influence education systems and policies worldwide
  • Advance your academic and research career

Application Process

Researchers interested in applying must follow the official submission process outlined on the LEVANTE platform.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Review eligibility and funding guidelines
  2. Prepare a detailed research proposal
  3. Submit through the official LEVANTE website

Applicants are encouraged to carefully align their proposals with LEVANTE’s research priorities and methodological expectations.

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by:

June 10, 2026

Late submissions will not be considered.

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the LEVANTE 2026 Call for Proposals.

Discover more global Grant opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post LEVANTE 2026 Call for Proposals: Up to $1 Million Funding for Global Research on Children’s Learning and Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026: Fully Funded Internship Opportunity for African Students and Graduates
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
If you are an ambitious student or recent graduate in Africa seeking a transformative career opportunity with a global brand, the Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026 is one of the most powerful internship programs currently open. Designed to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and real-world professional experience, this internship offers hands-on exposure, mentorship, and a […]

The post Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026: Fully Funded Internship Opportunity for African Students and Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are an ambitious student or recent graduate in Africa seeking a transformative career opportunity with a global brand, the Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026 is one of the most powerful internship programs currently open. Designed to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and real-world professional experience, this internship offers hands-on exposure, mentorship, and a potential pathway into full-time employment with Nestlé.

What is the Nestlé CWAR Nesternship Program?

The Nesternship is a flagship internship initiative by Nestlé designed to empower young African talent with practical work experience in a global corporate environment. It is part of Nestlé’s broader commitment to youth development under its “Needs YOUth” initiative.

Through this program, selected participants gain:

  • Hands-on experience working on real-life business projects
  • Direct mentorship from experienced professionals
  • Exposure to global business operations
  • Practical skills in key functional areas

The internship can be virtual or hybrid, allowing participants to work remotely or physically depending on location and company needs.

Why You Should Apply for the Nesternship

This is not just another internship—it is a career-launching platform. Here’s what makes it stand out:

1. Real-World Work Experience – Participants work on actual business projects across departments such as:

  • Finance and Accounting
  • Marketing and Communications
  • Sales
  • Engineering
  • Information Technology

2. Professional Mentorship – Interns are guided by industry experts who help shape their career paths and develop workplace-ready skills.

3. Paid Internship Opportunity – The program offers financial benefits including stipends and other rewards depending on the region.

4. Career Advancement Potential – Outstanding interns may gain opportunities for full-time roles or further engagement with Nestlé.

5. Flexible Work Structure – Participants can work remotely with just a computer and internet connection, making it accessible across Africa.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates)

The Nestlé CWAR Nesternship targets young Africans who are at the early stages of their careers.

Eligible Categories:

  • Undergraduate students
  • Recent graduates
  • Entry-level job seekers

Eligible Countries/Regions:

  • Countries within Central and West Africa, including:
    • Nigeria
    • Ghana
    • Côte d’Ivoire
    • Senegal
    • Burkina Faso
    • Other African countries within the CWAR region

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the Nesternship, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Be a full-time undergraduate student or recent graduate
  • Have a strong academic background (typically a minimum CGPA around 3.2 or equivalent)
  • Possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills
  • Demonstrate leadership potential and participation in extracurricular activities
  • Have access to a computer and reliable internet (for virtual roles)
  • Successfully complete the Talent Games assessment, a gamified evaluation process

Duration of the Internship

  • The program duration typically ranges from 1 month to 6 months, depending on availability and project requirements.

Benefits of the Nestlé CWAR Nesternship

Participants gain both tangible and intangible rewards, including:

Career Benefits:

  • Practical industry experience
  • Skill development in real business environments
  • Enhanced employability

Financial & Material Benefits:

  • Monthly stipend (varies by country)
  • Training and development resources
  • Internship completion certificate

Networking Opportunities:

  • Access to a global network of professionals
  • Collaboration with diverse teams

Application Process (Step-by-Step Guide)

Applying for the Nesternship involves a structured and competitive process:

Step 1: Online Registration

Applicants must sign up on the official Nestlé recruitment or Talent Games platform.

Step 2: Gamified Assessment

Candidates complete an interactive evaluation involving:

  • Case studies
  • Problem-solving tasks
  • Behavioral assessments

Step 3: Screening & Selection

Top-performing candidates are shortlisted and matched with suitable project teams.

Apply here

Key Skills You Will Develop

By participating in the Nesternship, you will gain:

  • Analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Communication and teamwork abilities
  • Leadership and project management experience
  • Industry-specific technical knowledge

Why This Opportunity Matters for African Youth

The Nestlé CWAR Nesternship addresses one of the biggest challenges facing young Africans today—the lack of practical work experience after graduation. By combining training, mentorship, and real-world exposure, the program helps bridge the gap between education and employment.

It is especially valuable in a competitive job market where employers prioritize experience alongside academic qualifications.

Deadline Information

  •  Not explicitly stated
  • However, applications are typically open for a short window (about 2 weeks in some cycles), making early application critical.

The Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026 is a highly competitive and career-defining internship opportunity for African students and graduates. With its strong focus on skill development, mentorship, and real-world experience, it provides a solid foundation for long-term professional success.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Nestlé CWAR Nesternship 2026: Fully Funded Internship Opportunity for African Students and Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Sawaba Fellowship 2026: Funded Opportunity for African Thinkers and Creatives
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Sawaba Fellowship offers a unique opportunity for African-based thinkers, artists, and organisers to receive funding, mentorship, and space to explore decolonial feminist ideas. Hosted by Liberation Alliance Africa, this three-month fellowship supports individuals committed to challenging colonial narratives and producing transformative knowledge rooted in African histories, memory, and lived realities. With a strong focus […]

The post Sawaba Fellowship 2026: Funded Opportunity for African Thinkers and Creatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Sawaba Fellowship offers a unique opportunity for African-based thinkers, artists, and organisers to receive funding, mentorship, and space to explore decolonial feminist ideas. Hosted by Liberation Alliance Africa, this three-month fellowship supports individuals committed to challenging colonial narratives and producing transformative knowledge rooted in African histories, memory, and lived realities. With a strong focus on interdisciplinary and practice-based work, the programme is designed for those seeking to deepen their impact while engaging in critical consciousness and collective reflection.

About the Program

The Sawaba Fellowship is an initiative of the Sawaba Institute of Critical Consciousness Raising, hosted by Liberation Alliance Africa. It brings together a small cohort of fellows from across the African continent to engage in research, storytelling, and creative practice that advances decolonial feminist futures.

The 2026 theme, “Not Pioneers of an Empty Land,” challenges dominant colonial narratives that erase indigenous histories, knowledge systems, and governance structures. Fellows are invited to interrogate and dismantle the myth that African societies lacked complexity prior to colonialism, instead centering ancestral memory, cultural continuity, and collective knowledge.

The fellowship begins with a fully funded in-person convening in May 2026, followed by a three-month period of project development and collaboration. Fellows are expected to produce original work aligned with the theme, which may take various forms including essays, research papers, artistic productions, exhibitions, or public engagements.

Why This Opportunity Matters

In a global landscape where colonial narratives continue to shape knowledge production and policy, opportunities like the Sawaba Fellowship are critical. They provide space for African voices to redefine frameworks of knowledge, identity, and justice on their own terms.

This fellowship goes beyond traditional academic or professional development programmes by prioritising lived experience, collective memory, and indigenous knowledge systems. It recognises that meaningful transformation requires not only new ideas but also a re-engagement with historical truths and suppressed narratives.

By supporting interdisciplinary work and community-rooted approaches, the programme contributes to broader efforts toward epistemic justice and sustainable, inclusive futures.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the Sawaba Fellowship will gain both tangible and intangible benefits that support their personal and professional growth.

Fellows receive a monthly stipend of USD 500, enabling them to focus on their projects without financial strain. The programme also includes a fully funded convening, offering a rare opportunity to connect in person with like-minded thinkers, artists, and organisers from across the continent.

Beyond financial support, fellows benefit from a collaborative learning environment that encourages critical dialogue, mentorship, and peer exchange. This creates a strong foundation for intellectual growth and long-term partnerships.

Participants also gain visibility for their work, as the fellowship supports the development of outputs that contribute to public discourse, whether through publications, artistic work, or community engagement. Ultimately, the experience equips fellows with the tools, networks, and confidence to continue advancing decolonial and feminist work beyond the programme.

Who Can Apply

The fellowship is open to individuals of African descent who are currently based on the African continent. Applicants may come from a wide range of disciplines and backgrounds, including academia, the arts, journalism, activism, and community organising.

Successful candidates are those who demonstrate a strong commitment to decolonial feminist politics, critical thinking, and social transformation. They should also be prepared to engage deeply with the fellowship theme and contribute meaningfully to collective learning spaces.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply, candidates must submit a complete application via email. This includes a cover letter, a project proposal outlining their intended work, a portfolio or CV showcasing relevant experience, and a draft budget.

Applications must be submitted by 24 April 2026. Due to the competitive nature of the fellowship, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Selected fellows must be available to attend the in-person convening in May 2026 and actively participate throughout the three-month programme.

Learn more about the program and requirements in the official description.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Sawaba Fellowship 2026: Funded Opportunity for African Thinkers and Creatives appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: $1.1 Million Gates Foundation President, Global Policy and Advocacy Job in Seattle, USA | Apply Before May 1, 2026
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Gates Foundation has announced an exceptional executive career opportunity for experienced global leaders. Applications are now open for the role of President, Global Policy and Advocacy, based in Seattle, USA, with a salary range of $800,000 to $1,100,000 USD annually. This is one of the highest-paying nonprofit leadership jobs currently available worldwide, offering the […]

The post $1.1 Million Gates Foundation President, Global Policy and Advocacy Job in Seattle, USA | Apply Before May 1, 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Gates Foundation has announced an exceptional executive career opportunity for experienced global leaders. Applications are now open for the role of President, Global Policy and Advocacy, based in Seattle, USA, with a salary range of $800,000 to $1,100,000 USD annually.

This is one of the highest-paying nonprofit leadership jobs currently available worldwide, offering the chance to shape global policy, strengthen international partnerships, and help solve some of the world’s biggest challenges including poverty, disease, and inequality.

About the Gates Foundation

The Gates Foundation is the world’s largest nonprofit organization focused on fighting poverty, disease, and inequity. Through investments, partnerships, and advocacy, the foundation works to ensure every person has the opportunity to live a healthy and productive life.

The organization is also known for offering world-class employee benefits, including:

  • Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage
  • No employee premiums
  • Generous paid time off
  • Paid family leave
  • Retirement contributions
  • Regional holidays
  • Employee communities and growth opportunities

About the Role

The President, Global Policy and Advocacy serves as the foundation’s top advisor on political, geopolitical, and government relations matters. This executive will work directly with the CEO, Governing Board, and senior leadership team to drive policy influence and build strategic partnerships around the world.

The selected candidate will lead the Global Policy and Advocacy division and oversee initiatives that advance the foundation’s priorities in global health, development, and U.S. domestic issues.

Key Responsibilities

1. Strategic Leadership

  • Advise executive leadership on global political developments
  • Guide decisions on risk management and policy direction
  • Support country-level strategies across multiple regions

2. Government Relations & Financing

  • Lead global government relations strategies
  • Build partnerships with donor governments and multilateral institutions
  • Support domestic resource mobilization and funding strategies

3. Global Partnerships

  • Build coalitions with governments, civil society, faith leaders, think tanks, and private sector stakeholders
  • Expand philanthropic giving in the U.S. and globally
  • Strengthen long-term support for health and development priorities

4. Public Representation

  • Represent the foundation at high-level global forums
  • Support strategic external engagements for senior leadership
  • Manage sensitive political relationships with professionalism and discretion

5. Organizational Leadership

  • Lead a globally distributed team
  • Align resources with long-term strategy
  • Ensure compliance, governance, and high performance across the division

Required Experience

Applicants should have:

  • Significant leadership experience in public policy, advocacy, diplomacy, or development
  • Deep understanding of global political systems
  • Experience managing large and complex international teams
  • Strong record of influencing public policy and institutional strategy
  • Exceptional communication and relationship-building skills

Preferred candidates may also have experience in:

  • Global health financing
  • Grantmaking
  • Strategic communications
  • Philanthropic partnerships
  • Investment portfolio management

Salary and Benefits

The salary for this executive role ranges from:

$800,000 – $1,100,000 USD per year

In addition to salary, employees receive an outstanding benefits package that supports health, family, retirement, and career growth.

Application Deadline

May 1, 2026

Late applications may not be considered, so interested candidates should apply as early as possible.

How to Apply

Applications are being managed through Russell Reynolds Associates.

Interested candidates should apply via the official platform using career code: 2604-021NA

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more job opportunities, click here.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This role is more than a job—it is a chance to influence global policy, reduce poverty, improve health outcomes, and help millions of people worldwide. For qualified senior leaders seeking impact-driven executive careers, this is a rare and prestigious opportunity.

The post $1.1 Million Gates Foundation President, Global Policy and Advocacy Job in Seattle, USA | Apply Before May 1, 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
